instruction_en: How do I know if my soil is good for farming?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na ɛkyerɛ sɛ m'asase yɛ ɔkorɔ wɔ okuafo adwuma mu?
response_en: Good farming soil is dark, crumbly, and has a pleasant earthy smell. Push your finger 5cm into the soil — if it holds shape but breaks apart easily, it is good. You can also send a sample to an agricultural office for a proper soil test.
response_tw: Asase pa wɔ okuafo adwuma mu yɛ ɛda, na ɛpepee yie, na ɛwɔ asase nhwiren foforo. Fa wo nsateaa hia asase no mu sɛntimita 5 — sɛ ɛkura ne ho nanso ɛpae yie a, ɛyɛ asase pa. Wobetumi de asase bi kɔ okuafo ofisi sɛ wɔhwɛ so yie.
prompt: Question: How do I know if my soil is good for farming?
completion: Good farming soil is dark, crumbly, and has a pleasant earthy smell. Push your finger 5cm into the soil — if it holds shape but breaks apart easily, it is good. You can also send a sample to an agricultural office for a proper soil test.

instruction_en: What type of fertilizer is best for maize?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ferefere bɛn na ɛho hia wɔ aburo dwumadi mu?
response_en: For maize, use NPK fertilizer (15-15-15) at planting, then top-dress with urea about 4-6 weeks after planting. Apply 2 bags of NPK per acre at planting and 1 bag of urea per acre when top-dressing.
response_tw: Aburo ho, fa NPK ferefere (15-15-15) de to asase mu bere a wode aburow to mu no. Afei fa urea de to so sɛntɔ 4-6 akyi a wode aburow to mu no. De NPK bags 2 to eka baako biara mu na urea bag 1 no bere a wode to so.

instruction_en: Can I use animal manure instead of chemical fertilizer?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Metumi de mmoa aguare afa ferefere ahemfie ho?
response_en: Yes, animal manure from cows, goats, or chickens is an excellent organic fertilizer. Apply well-composted manure 2-3 weeks before planting. It improves soil structure and provides slow-release nutrients.
response_tw: Aane, mmoa aguare fi boo, abirekyi, anaa akrɔma yɛ ferefere papa pa ara. De aguare a ɛawie wo yie no to asase so wiemu 2-3 ansa na wobede aba to mu. Ɛsiesie asase mu na ɛma aduan ho nhwiren ba bebree.

instruction_en: How do I make compost at home?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meyɛ compost wɔ fie?
response_en: Mix green materials like grass and vegetable waste with brown materials like dry leaves and straw in a pile. Keep it moist and turn it every 2 weeks. After 2-3 months, you will have rich compost ready for your farm.
response_tw: Fa nhwiren foforo te sɛ atɛntɛnni ne nnuan asennua ka bom ne nhwiren kɔkɔɔ te sɛ nnhaban kraen ne tow. Ma ɛnte na tware no mprenu biara. Abosome 2-3 akyi, wobɛnya compost pa a ɛawie wo yie wɔ wo afuo ho.

instruction_en: How do I improve sandy soil that dries out quickly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi yɛ asintiɛ asase a ɛyera ntɛm no yiye?
response_en: Add organic matter like compost, animal manure, or crop residues to sandy soil. This helps retain moisture and nutrients. Mulching the soil surface also reduces water evaporation. Consider drip irrigation for better water management.
response_tw: De nhwiren foforo te sɛ compost, mmoa aguare, anaa nnuaba ho nsennua ka asintiɛ asase no ho. Eyi boa ma nsukuro ne aduan ho nhwiren tena mu yie. Mulch a wode sɛɛ asase so no nso mma nsukyerɛ ba. Fa nsuo tete-tete kyɛ di dwuma sɛ wuhwɛ nsuo so yiye.

instruction_en: How do I prevent soil erosion on my farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mema asase amma ɛnhuru wɔ m'afuo mu?
response_en: Plant cover crops or grass along slopes, practice contour farming, build terraces on steep land, and mulch bare soil. Avoid leaving soil uncovered during heavy rain seasons.
response_tw: To nnuaba anaa atɛntɛnni wɔ kwantempon ho, yɛ contour farming, si terrace wɔ asase a ɛkwan so, na de mulch sɛɛ asase a ɛda. Bɔ hwɛ asase da ho akyɛ wɔ nsuo tenten bere mu.

instruction_en: My soil is too acidic. What can I do?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'asase yɛ acidic dodo. Dɛn na metumi yɛ?
response_en: Apply agricultural lime (calcium carbonate) to raise the pH of acidic soil. Typically apply 1-2 tonnes per hectare. Apply lime at least 3-4 weeks before planting and mix it well into the soil.
response_tw: De agricultural lime (calcium carbonate) to asase acidic no so sɛ ɛma pH no sor. Taa de tonne 1-2 to hectare biara so. De lime to asase so wiemu 3-4 ansa na wode aba to mu na ka ho yie wɔ asase mu.

instruction_en: What is the ideal soil pH for growing vegetables?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Asase pH bɛn na ɛyɛ ɔkorɔ ma nnuan?
response_en: Most vegetables grow best in soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0. Below 6.0 is too acidic and above 7.5 is too alkaline. You can test your soil pH using a simple pH testing kit from an agro-input shop or send a sample to your nearest agricultural laboratory.
response_tw: Nnuan bebree da sor pa wɔ asase a pH yɛ 6.0 ne 7.0 ntam. Ɛsua 6.0 a ɛyɛ acidic dodo na ɛboro 7.5 a ɛyɛ alkaline dodo. Wobetumi hwɛ wo asase pH wɔ pH testing kit fi agro-input adeɛtɔ hɔ anaa de asase bi kɔ agricultural laboratory a ɛbɛn wo.

instruction_en: What is green manure and how do I use it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na nhwiren-tew ferefere yɛ na ɛdeɛn na mefa di dwuma?
response_en: Green manure means growing specific crops like legumes or grasses and then plowing them back into the soil while still green. This adds organic matter and nitrogen to the soil. Common green manure crops include cowpea, mucuna, and lablab. Plow them in at least 3 weeks before planting your main crop.
response_tw: Nhwiren-tew ferefere kyerɛ kura nnuaba bi te sɛ abɔdweɛ anaa atɛntɛnni na afei boro wɔn gu asase mu bere a wɔda koraa. Eyi de nhwiren foforo ne nitrogen ka asase ho. Nhwiren-tew ferefere nnuaba a wɔtaa di dwuma ne abɔdweɛ, mucuna, ne lablab. Boro wɔn gu asase mu wiemu 3 ansa na wokura wo nnuaba titiriw.

instruction_en: How do I know my fertilizer is working?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim sɛ m'ferefere yɛ adwuma?
response_en: Signs that fertilizer is working include: darker green leaves, faster plant growth, larger leaves, and higher yield compared to unfertilized plots. If plants remain yellowish and grow slowly after 2-3 weeks of fertilizer application, the fertilizer may not have been applied correctly or the soil may have other problems.
response_tw: Ahohyenam sɛ ferefere yɛ adwuma ne: nhaban a wɔyɛ tuntum-akyen pa ara, nnuaba a wɔda sor ntɛm, nhaban kɛse, ne nnuaba a wɔda sor dodo sen beae a wɔanto ferefere. Sɛ nnuaba da hoho na wɔda sor brɛ akyi a ferefere to so wiemu 2-3 a, ferefere no beɛ to so yie anaa asase no wɔ nsɛm foforɔ.

instruction_en: Can too much fertilizer damage my crops?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ferefere dodo tumi sɛe m'nnuaba anaa?
response_en: Yes, over-fertilizing can burn plant roots and leaves, cause excessive leafy growth with poor fruiting, pollute water sources, and waste money. Always follow the recommended rates on fertilizer bags or advice from your extension officer. Start with less and increase gradually based on crop response.
response_tw: Aane, ferefere dodo tumi hyew nnuaba ntini ne nhaban, ma nnuaba nhaban da sor dodo na aba ba sua, ha nsuo mu, na seaa sika. Bue ferefere bag so krataa nhyɛso anaa fa wo extension officer nsɛm di. Fi ferefere sua na fa dodo so kakra kakra wɔ nnuaba tebea so.

instruction_en: What does NPK mean on fertilizer bags?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na NPK kyerɛ wɔ ferefere bag so?
response_en: NPK stands for Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), and Potassium (K) — the three main nutrients plants need. Nitrogen helps leafy growth, Phosphorus supports root development and flowering, and Potassium strengthens stems and improves fruit quality. The numbers like 15-15-15 show the percentage of each nutrient in the bag.
response_tw: NPK kyerɛ Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), ne Potassium (K) — aduan titiriw mmiɛnsa a nnuaba hia. Nitrogen boa nhaban da sor, Phosphorus boa ntini ne abode, na Potassium ma tibo yɛ den na aba yɛ pa. Nɔma te sɛ 15-15-15 no kyerɛ kɛse a aduan biara wɔ bag mu.

instruction_en: When should I NOT apply fertilizer?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn na nsɛ meto ferefere?
response_en: Do not apply fertilizer when: heavy rain is forecast as it will wash away the fertilizer, the soil is very dry as it can burn roots, plants are severely stressed from pests or disease, soil is waterlogged, or crops are very close to harvest. Always apply when soil has some moisture and weather is stable.
response_tw: Mto ferefere bere a: osu tenten rebɛba efisɛ ɛbɛhoro ferefere no kɔ, asase yera pa ara efisɛ betumi hyew ntini, nnuaba hia adwummakers anaa yadeɛ kɛse, nsuo hyɛ asase mu, anaa nnuaba rebɛwie ase ntɛm. To ferefere bere a asase wɔ nsuo kakra na ɛwim tebea yɛ pɛ.

completion: Do not apply fertilizer when: heavy rain is forecast as it will wash away the fertilizer, the soil is very dry as it can burn roots, plants are severely stressed from pests or disease, soil is waterlogged, or crops are very close to harvest. instruction_en: How do I test my soil at home without a kit?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehwɛ m'asase wɔ fie a menwɔ kit?
response_en: Simple home tests: for pH, mix soil with vinegar — if it fizzes the soil is alkaline. Mix soil with baking soda and water — if it fizzes the soil is acidic. For texture, squeeze moist soil — clay soil holds shape, sandy soil crumbles, and loam soil holds shape then crumbles. For organic matter, dark soil has more organic matter than pale soil.
response_tw: Fie hwɛ anammɔn ketewa: pH ho, ka asase ne vinegar — sɛ ɛpɔ a asase no yɛ alkaline. Ka asase ne baking soda ne nsuo — sɛ ɛpɔ a asase no yɛ acidic. Nsusuw ho, hia asase nte — amane asase kura ne ho, asintiɛ asase pae, na loam asase kura ne ho na afei pae. Nhwiren foforo ho, asase ɛda wɔ nhwiren foforo dodo sen asase hoho.

instruction_en: What causes soil to become hard and difficult to dig?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na ɛma asase yɛ den na ɛhia yiye?
response_en: Soil becomes hard from: overuse of heavy machinery which compacts the soil, lack of organic matter, repeatedly plowing at the same depth creating a hardpan layer, frequent flooding followed by drying, and low biological activity. To fix it, add organic matter, practice deep tillage occasionally, and use cover crops.
response_tw: Asase yɛ den fi: egyapade kɛse ho di dwuma dodo a ɛhia asase, nhwiren foforo aduan ho hia, bere biara boro asase sukyerɛ koro so a ɛyɛ hardpan, nsuo tenten a ɛto akyi na asase yera, ne nkwa a ɛdi asase mu hia. Sɛ wohwehwɛ siesie no a, de nhwiren foforo ka ho, bere bi boro asase mu tenten, na kura asase-bo nnuabas.

instruction_en: When is the best time to plant maize in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn na ɛyɛ ɔkorɔ sɛ wode aburow to mu wɔ Ghana?
response_en: In Ghana, the best time to plant maize is at the beginning of the rainy season — March to April in the south, and May to June in the north. Plant when you have received at least 2-3 good rains so the soil is well moistened.
response_tw: Wɔ Ghana, bere pa ara sɛ wode aburow to mu ne nsuo bere mfiase — Ɛbɔ kwan so Oforisuo wɔ apuei fam, na Ayɛwohomumo kwan so Mumu wɔ atifi fam. To aba mu bere a wanya nsu mmɛn 2-3 yie na asase no atenante yie.

instruction_en: How deep should I plant maize seeds?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛsukyerɛ bɛn na mede aburow aba to asase mu?
response_en: Plant maize seeds 3-5 cm deep. If the soil is dry, plant slightly deeper to reach moisture. Space seeds 25-30 cm apart within rows and 75-90 cm between rows.
response_tw: De aburow aba to asase mu sɛntimita 3-5. Sɛ asase no yera a, to mu kakra dodo na wo nsa adu nsukuro ho. Gyae asukyerɛ 25-30 sɛntimita wɔ aba biara ntam na 75-90 sɛntimita wɔ nnua so ntam.

instruction_en: What is crop rotation and why is it important?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dea ɛyɛ sɛ wosesa nnuaba gu asase mu na adɛn na ɛho hia?
response_en: Crop rotation means growing different crops on the same land each season. For example, grow maize in season 1, then cowpea in season 2. This breaks pest cycles, improves soil fertility, and increases yields.
response_tw: Nnuaba sesae kyerɛ sɛ wokura ahorow nnuaba wɔ asase koro so bere biara. Nhwɛso so, kura aburow bere 1 mu, afei kura abɔdweɛ wɔ bere 2 mu. Eyi bubu adwummakers ntam, yɛ asase mu aduan ho nhwiren na ɛma nnuaba da sor dodo.

instruction_en: How do I select good seeds for planting?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mepaw aba pa sɛ mede to mu?
response_en: Choose certified seeds from reputable agro-input dealers. Good seeds are uniform in size, free from damage, and have a high germination rate of at least 85%. Avoid using seeds from the previous harvest for hybrid varieties.
response_tw: Paw aba a wɔakyerɛw won fi agro-input atɔfo a wɔn din ayɛ pa. Aba pa yɛ pɛ wɔ boɔ mu, wɔ mfomso, na wɔn pue rate yɛ kɛse sen 85%. Bɔ hwɛ de aba a wogye fi dɛm nnuaba meto aba hybrid aba no ase.

instruction_en: How do I prepare a seedbed for planting?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mesiesie asase aba to mu ansa?
response_en: Clear the land of weeds and old crop residues. Plow or dig to loosen the soil to about 20-30 cm deep. Break up large clods and remove stones. Level the surface and apply basal fertilizer or compost before planting.
response_tw: Yi nhaban foforo ne nnuaba kedee nsennua fi asase no so. Boro anaa hu asase no kosi sɛntimita 20-30 mu. Bubu asase kɛse no na yi abo. Siesie so yie na de basal ferefere anaa compost to ansa na wode aba to mu.

instruction_en: Can I plant vegetables during the dry season?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Metumi to nnuan wɔ ɔpɛ bere mu?
response_en: Yes, you can grow vegetables in the dry season with irrigation. Crops like tomatoes, pepper, onions, leafy vegetables, and cucumbers grow well in dry season with proper watering.
response_tw: Aane, wobetumi kura nnuan wɔ ɔpɛ bere mu wɔ nsuo to so. Nnuaba te sɛ ntomato, mako, gyene, nhaban nnuan, ne cucumber da sor yie wɔ ɔpɛ bere mu nsuo to so a ɛyɛ yie.

instruction_en: What is the best way to transplant seedlings without killing them?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Anammɔn bɛn na ɛyɛ pa wɔ si nnuaba mfoforo ananmu a wɔnwu?
response_en: Water seedlings well the day before transplanting. Transplant in the late afternoon or on a cloudy day to reduce heat stress. Dig the hole bigger than the root ball. Remove seedling from container carefully without disturbing roots. Firm soil around roots and water immediately. Provide shade for 2-3 days if needed.
response_tw: De nsuo to nnuaba mfoforo yiye da a ɛdi ɛkan ansa na wosi wɔn ananmu. Si wɔn ananmu anwummere anaa da a owia nhye pa sɛ ɛma ahuhuro nkɔm wɔn. Hu tumtum kɛse sen ntini no. Yi nnuaba mfoforo fi nkuku mu pɛ a wonhaw ntini. Hia asase ntini ho yie na de nsuo to ntɛm. De ewim brɛ wɔn nnafoa 2-3 sɛ ɛhia.

instruction_en: How do I know if my seeds have germinated properly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim sɛ m'aba apue yie?
response_en: Check germination 5-10 days after planting depending on the crop. Good germination means at least 80% of planted seeds have sprouted uniformly. If germination is poor, check if seeds were planted too deep, soil was too dry or waterlogged, temperature was too extreme, or seeds were old. Replant gaps with fresh seeds immediately.
response_tw: Hwɛ aba pue nnafoa 5-10 akyi a wode aba to mu no wɔ nnuaba no so. Aba pue yie kyerɛ sɛ aba a wode to mu no 80% pɛ pue bere koro. Sɛ aba pue nyɛ yie a, hwɛ sɛ wode aba to asase mu tenten dodo, asase yera anaa nsuo hyɛ mu, osu yɛ dodo anaa huru dodo, anaa aba no da. Fa aba foforo to tumtum a wɔwɔ mu ntɛm.

instruction_en: What is a nursery and when should I use one?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na nursery yɛ na bere bɛn na ɛsɛ me di?
response_en: A nursery is a protected area where seeds are germinated and young plants are raised before transplanting to the main field. Use a nursery for: small expensive seeds like tomato, pepper, and onion; crops that need a head start before rains; and crops where seedling survival in the field is uncertain. Nurseries save seeds and improve stand establishment.
response_tw: Nursery yɛ beae a wɔhwɛ so a wɔde aba kɔ mu na wɔkura nnuaba mfoforo ansa na wɔsi wɔn ananmu wɔ afuo titiriw. Fa nursery di sɛ: aba ketewa a ɛboɔ yɛ den te sɛ ntomato, mako, ne gyene; nnuaba a wɔhia bere ansa na osu reba; ne nnuaba a ɛnni adanse sɛ wɔbɛtena wɔ afuo mu. Nursery kora aba na ɛma nnuaba si yie.

instruction_en: Can I mix different vegetables in the same garden?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Metumi ka nnuan ahodoɔ wɔ afuo koro mu anaa?
response_en: Yes, mixing vegetables is called companion planting and can be very beneficial. Good combinations include tomatoes with basil (basil repels tomato pests), maize with beans and squash (the Three Sisters method), onions with carrots (they repel each other's pests), and marigolds with any vegetable (repels many insects). Avoid planting crops from the same family together as they share pests.
response_tw: Aane, nnuan ka bom sɛ companion planting na ɛtumi yɛ boa pa. Nnuaba a wɔyɛ adwuma yie wɔ bom ne ntomato ne basil (basil kora ntomato adwummakers ase), aburow ne abɔdweɛ ne squash (Three Sisters anammɔn), gyene ne carrot (wɔkora wɔn ho adwummakers ase), na marigold ne nnuan biara (ɛkora adwummakers bebree ase). Bɔ hwɛ kura nnuaba a wɔfi abusua koro wɔ faako efisɛ wɔnya adwummakers koro.

instruction_en: How do I plant on a slope to prevent runoff?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meto aba wɔ kwantempon so sɛ nsuo anto kɔ?
response_en: On slopes, always plant across the slope (contour planting) rather than up and down. Make ridges along contour lines to hold water. Plant cover crops or grasses on the steepest parts. Use tied ridges (ridges with cross-ties every few meters) to capture rainwater. Mulch heavily to protect soil between plants.
response_tw: Wɔ kwantempon so, to aba twa kwantempon so daa (contour planting) na mmom kɔ sor na gu ase. Yɛ ntwaho wɔ contour lines ho sɛ ɛkora nsuo. To asase-bo nnuabas anaa atɛntɛnni wɔ beae a ɛkwan pa ara. Fa tied ridges (ntwaho a wɔne kyɛ ɛtɔ so baako biara) sɛ ɛgye osu nsuo. De mulch to so papaapa sɛ ɛkora asase nnuaba ntam.

instruction_en: What is the right age to transplant tomato seedlings?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Mfeɛ bɛn na ɛfata sɛ mesi ntomato nnuaba mfoforo ananmu?
response_en: Transplant tomato seedlings when they are 4-6 weeks old, have 4-6 true leaves, are 15-20 cm tall, and have a sturdy stem. Seedlings that are too young may not survive transplanting shock. Seedlings that are too old (over 8 weeks) become root-bound and may not establish well in the field.
response_tw: Si ntomato nnuaba mfoforo ananmu bere a wɔdin nnawotwe 4-6, wɔwɔ nhaban 4-6, wɔn soroɔ yɛ sɛntimita 15-20, na wɔwɔ tibo den. Nnuaba mfoforo a wɔkum dodo betumi ankyɛ si ananmu no ɔkɔm. Nnuaba mfoforo a wɔadin dodo (nnawotwe 8 kenkan) yɛ root-bound na betumi ansi yie wɔ afuo mu.

Seedlings that are too old (over 8 weeks) become root-bound and may not establish well in the field.

instruction_en: What is raised bed farming and what are its benefits?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na asase-sor afuo farming yɛ na mfaaso bɛn na ɛwɔ?
response_en: Raised bed farming means growing crops on elevated soil mounds or frames above the ground level. Benefits include better drainage, improved soil structure, easier weeding and harvesting, higher yield per square meter, better pest management, and ability to farm in poor soil areas. It is ideal for small spaces and home gardens.
response_tw: Asase-sor afuo farming kyerɛ kura nnuaba wɔ asase mounts anaa frame a ɛda sor sen asase soronko. Mfaaso ho ne: nsuo pue mu yie, asase mu siesie pa, nhaban tia ne nnuaba gye yɛ mmerɛ, nnuaba da sor dodo wɔ square meter biara mu, adwummakers hwɛ ase yie, na tumi kura wɔ asase a enni pa mu. Ɛfata afuo ketewa ne fie afuo.

instruction_en: How do I grow crops in containers or pots if I have no land?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mekura nnuaba wɔ nkuku anaa pots mu sɛ menni asase?
response_en: Growing in containers is called container gardening. Use large buckets, old tires, plastic bags, or purpose-made pots. Fill with a mix of soil, compost, and sand for good drainage. Good container crops include tomatoes, peppers, leafy vegetables, herbs, and onions. Place in a sunny spot and water frequently as containers dry out faster than ground soil.
response_tw: Kura wɔ nkuku mu sɛ container gardening. Fa bucket kɛse, tayɛ kedee, plastik bags, anaa nkuku a wɔyɛ wɔn ho. Hye asase, compost, ne asintiɛ bom mu sɛ nsuo pue mu yie. Nnuaba a wɔda sor yie wɔ nkuku mu ne ntomato, mako, nhaban nnuan, nduaba aduan, ne gyene. Si wɔn wɔ beae a owia hye na de nsuo to pɛ efisɛ nkuku yera ntɛm sen asase.

instruction_en: My maize leaves have holes and are being eaten. What should I do?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'aburow nhaban wɔ tumtum na wɔredidi won. Dɛn na meyɛ?
response_en: This is likely the Fall Armyworm. Look inside the whorl for worms. Apply insecticides like emamectin benzoate early morning or late evening. Sand mixed with ash applied into the whorl also helps as an organic option.
response_tw: Eyi tumi yɛ Aborɔnoma Adwummaker Kɛse adwummaker. Hwɛ nhaban mu sɛ wɔhwehwɛ adwummakers anaa wɔ ho ntama. De adwummakers dawuro te sɛ emamectin benzoate guare owia da ano anaa nnɔnhwerew mu. Asintiɛ a waka nkamfo ka ho no tumi de gu nhaban mu sɛ nhwiren dawuro.

instruction_en: How do I control weevils in stored grains?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi de weevils hyɛ ase wɔ m'aduan a maguina mu?
response_en: Dry the grain properly before storage to below 13% moisture. Use hermetic airtight bags which suffocate insects. You can also mix grain with diatomite dust or apply approved grain protectant chemicals.
response_tw: Yaw aduan no yie ansa na waguina mu na ɛsua 13% nsuku. Fa hermetic bags a wɔapo so yie a ɛkum adwummakers wɔ mu. Wobetumi de aduan ka diatomite tukutuku ho anaa de aduan adwummakers dawuro a wɔagyei no to so.
prompt: Question: How do I control weevils in stored grains?
completion: Dry the grain properly before storage to below 13% moisture. Use hermetic airtight bags which suffocate insects. You can also mix grain with diatomite dust or apply approved grain protectant chemicals.

instruction_en: My tomato plants are wilting even though I water them. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'ntomato nnua rewo na mede nsu hyɛ won. Dɛn na ɛhia?
response_en: Wilting despite watering could be bacterial wilt or fusarium wilt — soil-borne diseases. Cut the stem — if the inside is brown or oozes liquid, it is wilt disease. Remove infected plants and rotate crops for 2-3 years.
response_tw: Nwonwon a ɛba wɔ nsuo a wode hyɛ won akyi no betumi yɛ bacteria nwiinwii yadeɛ anaa fusarium yadeɛ wilt — yadeɛ a ɛfiri asase mu. Twa tibo no — sɛ mu yɛ ɛda anaa nsuo pue a, ɛyɛ wilt yadeɛ. Yi nnua a wɔayarɛ no fi afuo mu na sesa nnuaba mfe 2-3.

instruction_en: My cassava leaves are turning yellow and curling. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'bankye nhaban redan sika na rekyekye. Dɛn na ɛhia?
response_en: This could be Cassava Mosaic Disease spread by whiteflies. Symptoms include yellowing and leaf curling. There is no cure — remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Plant resistant varieties and control whitefly populations.
response_tw: Eyi betumi yɛ Cassava Mosaic Yadeɛ a fitaa abowa adwummakers de brɛ. Ahohyenam ho ne nhaban hoho ne kyekyere. Enni dawuro — yi nnua a wɔayarɛ no na seaa won ntɛm ara. To aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase na kora fitaa abowa adwummakers ase.
prompt: Question: My cassava leaves are turning yellow and curling. What is wrong?
completion: This could be Cassava Mosaic Disease spread by whiteflies. Symptoms include yellowing and leaf curling. There is no cure — remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Plant resistant varieties and control whitefly populations.

instruction_en: What is Fall Armyworm and how dangerous is it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na Aborɔnoma Adwummaker Kɛse ne ɛyɛ ɔkɔmm bɛn?
response_en: Fall Armyworm is a highly destructive caterpillar that mainly attacks maize. It can destroy an entire field within days. Control with insecticides like emamectin benzoate, apply early morning, and target the whorls where larvae hide.
response_tw: Aborɔnoma Adwummaker Kɛse yɛ aborɔnoma adwummaker a ɔsɛe dodo na ɔtaa kɔ aburow so. Ɔtumi seaa afuo koro nyinaa wɔ nnafoa mu. Kora ase wɔ adwummakers dawuro te sɛ emamectin benzoate, de to owia da ano, na hyɛ nhaban mu a adwummakers no kɔfa ho.

instruction_en: How do I control aphids on my crops?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi kora aphid ase wɔ m'nnuaba so?
response_en: Aphids are small soft insects that cluster on young shoots. Spray them off with a strong jet of water, or apply neem oil, insecticidal soap, or systemic insecticides like imidacloprid. Introduce natural predators like ladybirds if possible.
response_tw: Aphid yɛ adwummakers ketewa a wɔhyia wɔ nnuaba mfoforo so. De nsuo kɛse guare won afi, anaa de neem dawuro, adwummakers dawuro sabɔ, anaa adwummakers dawuro te sɛ imidacloprid gu won so. De aborɔkyir adwummakers a ɔdi wɔn te sɛ ladybird fa ba sɛ wonya tumi.

instruction_en: What causes dark spots on my tomato fruits?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na ɛma m'ntomato aba so wɔ ntuntum nsono?
response_en: Dark spots at the bottom of tomatoes are Blossom End Rot caused by calcium deficiency and irregular watering. Maintain consistent watering and apply calcium-rich fertilizers. Dark spots on the sides may be from fungal diseases.
response_tw: Ntuntum nsono a ɛwɔ ntomato aba ase no yɛ Blossom End Rot, a calcium hia na nsuo to nsɛ ama. Kora nsuo to pɛ na de calcium ferefere to so. Ntuntum nsono a ɛwɔ afã so betumi fi fungal yadeɛ.

instruction_en: My groundnuts have spots on the leaves. What disease is this?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'nkatie nhaban wɔ nsono. Yadeɛ bɛn na ɛyɛ?
response_en: Leaf spots on groundnuts are usually Early Leaf Spot or Late Leaf Spot fungal diseases. Spray with fungicides like mancozeb or chlorothalonil every 2 weeks. Plant resistant varieties and practice crop rotation.
response_tw: Nhaban nsono wɔ nkatie so taa yɛ Early Nhaban Nsono Yadeɛ anaa Late Nhaban Nsono Yadeɛ fungal yadeɛ. De mancozeb anaa chlorothalonil guare mmere 2 biara. To aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase na yɛ nnuaba sesae.

instruction_en: How do I prevent pests in my vegetable garden?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mema adwummakers amma wɔnba m'nnuan afuo mu?
response_en: Practice crop rotation, remove crop residues after harvest, use pest-resistant varieties, plant trap crops like marigolds, use nets or row covers, and monitor your farm regularly.
response_tw: Yɛ nnuaba sesae, yi nnuaba nsennua akyi a wogye, fa aba a ɛtumi adwummakers ase, to trap crops te sɛ marigold sɛ ɛtwe adwummakers fi nnuaba pa ho, fa nets anaa row covers, na hwɛ wo afuo so pɛ.

instruction_en: How do I control grasshoppers on my farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi kora totrobonini ase wɔ m'afuo so?
response_en: Control grasshoppers by early morning spraying with approved insecticides like lambda-cyhalothrin. Biological control using Metarhizium fungi is also effective. Community-wide spraying is more effective than individual farm treatment.
response_tw: Wobetumi kora totrobonini ase wɔ owia da ano guare adwummakers dawuro a wɔagyei te sɛ lambda-cyhalothrin. Metarhizium fungi biological control nso yɛ adwuma pa. Oman nyinaa guare yɛ adwuma pa sen afuo baako biara.

instruction_en: What are the whiteflies on my vegetables and how do I get rid of them?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Adwummakers fitaa a wɔwɔ m'nnuan so yɛ dɛn na metumi yi wɔn?
response_en: Whiteflies are tiny white insects that suck plant sap and spread viruses. Control them by spraying insecticidal soap, neem oil, or imidacloprid. Yellow sticky traps also help. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer which attracts them.
response_tw: Fitaa abowa yɛ adwummakers fitaa ketewa a wɔnom nnuaba mogya na wɔde yadeɛ brɛ. Kora wɔn ase wɔ adwummakers dawuro sabɔ, neem dawuro, anaa imidacloprid so. Yellow sticky traps nso boa. Bɔ hwɛ nitrogen ferefere dodo efisɛ ɛtwe wɔn.

instruction_en: Birds are eating my sorghum and millet. How do I protect my crop?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Anomaa redidi m'ɔtɛ ne ɔtɛ pa. Ɛdeɛn na mekora m'nnuaba?
response_en: Use bird scaring methods: move scarecrows regularly to remain effective, hang reflective tape or old CDs around the field, use noise-makers, and recruit children to chase birds during grain-filling stage. Growing early-maturing varieties reduces the bird damage period.
response_tw: Fa anomaa kora anammɔn: tware anomaa kyerɛma pɛ sɛ ɛyɛ adwuma yie, si reflective tape anaa CD kedee wɔ afuo ho, fa nne adeɛ, na fa mmofra boa sɛ wɔtaa anomaa no bere a aba no repuo. Kura aba a ɛwie ntɛm maa anomaa sɛe bere sua.

instruction_en: How do I control mango mealybug?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi kora mangoro mealybug ase?
response_en: Mango mealybugs appear as white cottony masses on stems and fruits. Control by pruning heavily infested branches, applying insecticidal soap or neem oil, banding tree trunks with sticky tape to prevent crawlers from climbing, and introducing natural predators like Cryptolaemus beetles.
response_tw: Mangoro mealybug ba sɛ dodow fitaa wɔ nnua tiboo ne aba so. Kora wɔn ase wɔ twa ntini a wɔhyia so kɛse, de adwummakers dawuro sabɔ anaa neem dawuro gu so, de sticky tape kaa ntini ho, na de Cryptolaemus beetle a ɔdi wɔn fa ba.

instruction_en: My beans have rust disease. How do I treat it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'abɔdweɛ wɔ rust yadeɛ. Ɛdeɛn na meyɛ?
response_en: Bean rust appears as orange-brown powdery spots on leaves. Remove and destroy severely infected leaves. Spray with fungicides containing mancozeb or sulfur. Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead watering which spreads spores.
response_tw: Bean rust ba sɛ nsono ɛda-bɔɔ wɔ nhaban so. Yi na seaa nhaban a wɔayarɛ kɛse. De mancozeb anaa sulfur fungicide guare. To aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase. Bɔ hwɛ nsuo guare firi sor efisɛ ɛde yadeɛ tete.

instruction_en: Striga witch weed is killing my sorghum. What can I do?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Striga nhaban foforo resɛe m'ɔtɛ. Dɛn na metumi yɛ?
response_en: Striga is a parasitic weed that attaches to crop roots. Control includes using Striga-resistant varieties, applying Imazapyr-coated seeds, hand pulling Striga before it flowers, growing trap crops like Sudan grass, and applying fertilizer to help crops outcompete Striga.
response_tw: Striga yɛ nhaban foforo a ɔkyerɛ ne ho wɔ nnuaba ntini so. Kora ase wɔ de aba a ɛtumi Striga ase hyɛ, de Imazapyr-coated aba, twe Striga wɔ nsa ansa na ɔba abode, kura trap crops te sɛ Sudan grass, na de ferefere to sɛ ɛboa nnuaba no nko ɔkan.

instruction_en: How do I safely use pesticides on my farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mefa adwummakers dawuro di dwuma a ɛho tumi wɔ m'afuo mu?
response_en: Always read the label before use. Wear protective clothing including gloves, boots, mask, and long sleeves. Mix pesticides in open air away from water sources. Never eat, drink, or smoke while handling pesticides. Store in original containers away from children.
response_tw: Kenkan krataa a ɛwɔ so ansa na wodi dwuma. Hye ntoma a ɛkora wo ho te sɛ nsatea nhoma, mbotɔ, aboa, ne ntama tenten. Ka dawuro no wɔ baabi a mframa wɔ hɔ tete fi nsuo ho. Mmu aduan, nnom nsu, anaa mfa sigaret bere a wode dawuro no di dwuma.

instruction_en: What is aflatoxin and how does it affect my crop?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na aflatoxin dze yɛ na ɛyɛ m'nnuaba dɛn?
response_en: Aflatoxin is a toxic substance produced by molds that grow on grains, groundnuts, and spices when stored in humid conditions. It is harmful to humans and animals and causes liver disease. Prevent it by drying crops properly and using clean airtight storage.
response_tw: Aflatoxin Dze yɛ nsɛm a ɛyɛ ɔkɔm a fungus a ɛda sor wɔ nnuaba, nkatie, ne nduaba so bere a wɔaguina wɔ huhu mu na ɛba. Ɛyɛ onipa ne mmoa ɔkɔm na ɛma afu yadeɛ. Kora ase wɔ yaw nnuaba yie ansa na waguina na de nkuku a apo so yie guina.

instruction_en: What is integrated pest management (IPM)?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na adwummakers hwɛ anammɔn kaa bom (IPM) yɛ?
response_en: Integrated Pest Management (IPM) is an approach that combines multiple pest control methods to minimize chemical use. It includes: monitoring crops regularly to catch problems early, using resistant varieties, practicing good agronomic practices, using biological controls like beneficial insects, and only using chemicals as a last resort when pest populations exceed economic thresholds.
response_tw: Adwummakers Hwɛ Anammɔn Kaa Bom (IPM) yɛ anammɔn a wɔka adwummakers kora anammɔn bebree bom sɛ chemical fa sua. Mu wɔ: hwɛ nnuaba so pɛ sɛ wuhu nsɛm ntɛm, fa aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase, yɛ okuafo adwuma pa, fa biological controls te sɛ adwummakers a wɔboa, na fa chemicals pɛ bere a adwummakers a ɛyɛ dodo sene sika mu a wobɛnya.

instruction_en: How do I make homemade organic insecticide from chili pepper?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meyɛ fie nhwiren adwummakers dawuro fi mako?
response_en: Blend 10-15 hot chili peppers with 1 litre of water. Strain through cloth, add a little liquid soap, and dilute with 5 more litres of water. Spray on affected plants in the morning or evening. The capsaicin in chili repels and kills soft-bodied insects like aphids and mites. Reapply every 3-4 days and after rain.
response_tw: Blend mako hye 10-15 ne nsuo litre 1 bom. Hun wɔ ntama mu, de liquid soap kakra ka ho, na fa nsuo litre 5 bio ka ho. Guare nnuaba a wɔayarɛ so owia da ano anaa anwummere. Capsaicin a ɛwɔ mako mu kora adwummakers a wɔn ho yɛ mmerɛ te sɛ aphid ne mite ase. To so bio nnafoa 3-4 biara na osu akyi.

instruction_en: What are nematodes and how do they affect my crops?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na nematode yɛ na ɛyɛ m'nnuaba dɛn?
response_en: Nematodes are microscopic worms that live in soil. Some are harmful to crops — they attack roots causing galls (swellings), stunted growth, and yellowing. They are common in sandy soils and warm climates. Control by rotating crops, using resistant varieties, adding organic matter to soil, and solarizing soil (covering with clear plastic to heat it and kill nematodes during the dry season).
response_tw: Nematode yɛ nwirɛntwi a wɔte asase mu a wɔhunu da. Bi yɛ nnuaba ɔkɔm — wɔtwe ntini ho ma ɛyɛ kɛse, nnuaba nni sor yie, na nhaban hoho. Wɔtaa wɔ asintiɛ asase ne ahuhuro mu. Kora ase wɔ sesa nnuaba, fa aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase, de nhwiren foforo ka asase ho, na solarize asase (fa plastik fitaa sɛɛ so sɛ ɛhye mu na kum nematode wɔ ɔpɛ bere mu).
prompt: Question: What are nematodes and how do they affect my crops?
completion: Nematodes are microscopic worms that live in soil. Some are harmful to crops — they attack roots causing galls (swellings), stunted growth, and yellowing. They are common in sandy soils and warm climates. Control by rotating crops, using resistant varieties, adding organic matter to soil, and solarizing soil (covering with clear plastic to heat it and kill nematodes during the dry season).

Kora ase wɔ sesa nnuaba, fa aba a ɛtumi hyɛ ase, de nhwiren foforo ka asase ho, na solarize asase (fa plastik fitaa sɛɛ so sɛ ɛhye mu na kum nematode wɔ ɔpɛ bere mu).

instruction_en: What is powdery mildew and how do I control it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na tukutuku fitaa yadeɛ yɛ na ɛdeɛn na metumi kora ase?
response_en: Powdery mildew appears as white powdery patches on leaves and stems. It is caused by fungi and thrives in warm, dry conditions with high humidity. It weakens plants and reduces yield. Control by: removing infected leaves, improving air circulation by pruning, spraying with diluted baking soda solution (1 tablespoon per litre of water), neem oil, or sulfur-based fungicides.
response_tw: Tukutuku fitaa yadeɛ ba sɛ fitaa tukutuku a ɛwɔ nhaban ne tibo so. Fungus ama na ɛda sor pa wɔ ahuhuro mu a ɛwɔ huhu. Ɛma nnuaba yɛ mmerɛ na eba nnuaba a wɔda sor sua. Kora ase wɔ: yi nhaban a wɔayarɛ, ma mframa pue mu yie wɔ twa nnua so, guare baking soda a wɔasusu mu (tablespoon baako wɔ nsuo litre biara mu), neem dawuro, anaa sulfur-based fungicide.

instruction_en: My crop leaves look burnt at the tips. What is wrong?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: M'nnuaba nhaban ɔkyɛ yɛ sɛ wɔahyew. Dɛn na ɛhia?
response_en: Leaf tip burn can be caused by: fertilizer burn from too much nitrogen or potassium, salt stress from poor quality irrigation water, calcium deficiency, water stress during hot weather, or wind damage. Check your fertilizer rates and water quality first. Apply calcium fertilizer and ensure consistent watering. The condition often improves once the cause is corrected.
response_tw: Nhaban ɔkyɛ hyew betumi fi: ferefere burn fi nitrogen anaa potassium dodo, salt ɔkɔm fi nsuo a enni pa, calcium hia, nsuo hia wɔ ahuhuro mu, anaa mframa ɔkɔm. Hwɛ wo ferefere kɛse ne nsuo ho pa ansa. De calcium ferefere to so na kora nsuo to pɛ. Tebea no taa yɛ pa bere a wuyɛ dawuro.

instruction_en: How do I identify and control spider mites?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehunu spider mite na metumi kora wɔn ase?
response_en: Spider mites are tiny red or brown creatures barely visible to the naked eye. Signs include: fine webbing on leaves and stems, tiny yellow or white dots on leaves (feeding damage), and leaves turning bronze or yellow. Control by spraying with strong water jet, neem oil, insecticidal soap, or miticides. Mites thrive in hot and dry conditions — adequate watering helps prevent them.
response_tw: Spider mite yɛ adwummakers ɛda-kɔkɔɔ anaa ɛda-bɔɔ ketewa a wohunu wɔn yie. Ahohyenam ho ne: nwirɛntwi a ɛwɔ nhaban ne tibo so, nsono hoho anaa fitaa ketewa ketewa wɔ nhaban so (adidi akyinnye), na nhaban a wɔredan kɔkɔɔ anaa hoho. Kora ase wɔ de nsuo kɛse guare, neem dawuro, adwummakers dawuro sabɔ, anaa miticide. Mite da sor pa wɔ ahuhuro ne ɔpɛ bere — nsuo pa boa sɛ wɔnba.

instruction_en: What causes root rot and how do I prevent it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na ɛma ntini sɛe na ɛdeɛn na metumi kora ase?
response_en: Root rot is caused by soil-borne fungi, mainly when soil stays waterlogged for too long. It causes roots to turn brown and mushy, plants to wilt even when soil is wet, and eventual plant death. Prevent by: improving soil drainage, avoiding overwatering, not planting in low-lying waterlogged areas, using raised beds, treating seeds with fungicide before planting, and rotating crops.
response_tw: Ntini sɛe fi asase fungus, titiriw bere a nsuo da asase mu tenten dodo. Ɛma ntini dan ɛda ne mmerɛ, nnuaba nwo bere a asase nte, na nnua wu. Kora ase wɔ: siesie asase nsuo pue anammɔn, bɔ hwɛ nsuo to dodo, mto nnuaba wɔ asase ase a nsuo hia mu, fa asase-sor afuo, de aba ka fungicide ho ansa na woto mu, na sesa nnuaba.

instruction_en: How do I control thrips on my onions?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi kora thrips ase wɔ m'gyene so?
response_en: Thrips are tiny insects that feed on onion leaves causing silvery streaks and distorted growth. Control by: removing heavily infested leaves, spraying with neem oil or insecticides like spinosad or imidacloprid, mulching to prevent thrips from pupating in soil, avoiding planting near other infested crops, and using blue sticky traps for monitoring.
response_tw: Thrips yɛ adwummakers ketewa a wɔdi gyene nhaban so ma ɛkyerɛ silver ne nnuaba a wɔnni sor yie. Kora ase wɔ: yi nhaban a wɔhyia so kɛse, guare neem dawuro anaa adwummakers dawuro te sɛ spinosad anaa imidacloprid, de mulch to sɛ thrips ansi asase mu, bɔ hwɛ to nnuaba bɛn nnuaba foforɔ a wɔahyia so, na fa blue sticky traps hwɛ wɔn so.

instruction_en: What is damping off disease in seedlings?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na nnuaba-mfoforo wu yadeɛ yadeɛ yɛ wɔ nnuaba mfoforo mu?
response_en: Damping off is a fungal disease that causes seedlings to suddenly collapse and die, usually at the soil level where the stem meets the ground. The stem appears water-soaked and constricted. It is caused by overwatering, poor drainage, overcrowded seedlings, and unsterilized potting mix. Prevent by using sterilized soil, avoiding overwatering, ensuring good air circulation, and treating seeds with fungicide.
response_tw: Nnuaba-mfoforo wu yadeɛ yɛ fungal yadeɛ a ɛma nnuaba mfoforo tu ntɛm na wɔwu, titiriw wɔ asase so a tibo ne asase hyia hɔ. Tibo no yɛ sɛ nsuo da mu na ɛhyia. Nsuo dodo, nsuo pue anammɔn a enni pa, nnuaba mfoforo a wɔhyia so, ne potting mix a wonsi amu ama. Kora ase wɔ fa asase a wɔasi amu, bɔ hwɛ nsuo dodo, kora mframa pue mu yie, na de aba ka fungicide ho ansa na woto.

instruction_en: How do I identify if my crop has a nutrient deficiency?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehunu sɛ m'nnuaba hia aduan?
response_en: Common deficiency signs: Nitrogen deficiency — older leaves turn yellow starting from the bottom of the plant. Iron deficiency — young leaves turn yellow but veins stay green. Phosphorus deficiency — leaves turn purple or reddish. Potassium deficiency — leaf edges turn brown and crispy. Calcium deficiency — new leaves distort and curl. A soil test will confirm which nutrients are lacking.
response_tw: Aduan hia ahohyenam a wɔtaa hu: Nitrogen hia — nhaban kedee a wɔwɔ nnua ase hoho fi nnua ase. Iron hia — nhaban foforo hoho nanso nhwiren ho da tuntum-akyen. Phosphorus hia — nhaban dan purpura anaa kɔkɔɔ. Potassium hia — nhaban ho dan ɛda ne den. Calcium hia — nhaban foforo pae ne kyekye. Asase hwɛ bɛkyerɛ aduan a ehia.

instruction_en: How do I control the tomato leaf miner?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi kora ntomato nhaban miner ase?
response_en: Tomato leaf miners are larvae that tunnel inside leaves creating winding white trails. Control by: removing and destroying heavily mined leaves, applying spinosad or abamectin insecticides, using yellow sticky traps to catch adult flies, releasing parasitic wasps as biological control, and avoiding planting tomatoes after tomatoes in the same location.
response_tw: Ntomato nhaban miner yɛ larvae a wɔkɔ nhaban mu mu na wɔyɛ kwan fitaa a ahwimhwim. Kora ase wɔ: yi ne seaa nhaban a wɔahyia so kɛse, de spinosad anaa abamectin guare, fa yellow sticky traps gye sorɔ a ɛyɛ hogua mu no, de parasitic wasp guare sɛ biological control, na bɔ hwɛ to ntomato akyi ntomato wɔ faako koro.

instruction_en: What is citrus greening disease and can it be cured?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na citrus greening yadeɛ yɛ na wobetumi ayɛ dawuro?
response_en: Citrus greening (also called Huanglongbing or HLB) is a deadly bacterial disease spread by the Asian citrus psyllid insect. Symptoms include yellowing leaves, misshapen bitter fruits, and tree decline. There is currently no cure — infected trees should be removed and destroyed to prevent spread. Control psyllid insects and use certified disease-free planting material.
response_tw: Citrus greening (a wɔfre no Huanglongbing anaa HLB) yɛ bacterial yadeɛ a ɛkum na Asian citrus psyllid adwummaker de tete. Ahohyenam ho ne nhaban hoho, aba a asɛe ne a wɔn dɛm yɛ den, na nnua hia. Enni dawuro saa ara — yi nnua a wɔayarɛ na seaa wɔn sɛ yadeɛ anntete. Kora psyllid adwummakers ase na fa aba a wɔakyerɛw wɔ ho to mu.

instruction_en: How much water do my crops need?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Nsuo bɛn na m'nnuaba hia?
response_en: Most crops need about 25-50mm of water per week. Signs of water stress include wilting and dry soil. In the dry season, water deeply but less frequently to encourage deep rooting. Early morning watering reduces evaporation.
response_tw: Nnuaba bebree hia nsuo sɛntimita 25-50 dapɛn biara mu. Nsuo hia ahohyenam ho ne nwonwon ne asase a ɛyera. Ɔpɛ bere mu, de nsuo to asase mu kɛse nanso mmere pii bi no de nsuo to ho sɛ nnua no na wɔde wɔn ntini kɔ ase yie. Owia da ano nsuo guare mma nsukyerɛ yɛ dodo.

instruction_en: How do I know when my crops need watering?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim bere a m'nnuaba hia nsuo?
response_en: Push your finger 5cm deep into the soil — if dry, water is needed. Watch for early morning wilting as a sign of drought stress. Sandy soils need watering more often than clay soils. Tomatoes and vegetables need more frequent watering than cassava.
response_tw: Hia wo nsateaa asase mu sɛntimita 5 — sɛ ɛyera a, hia nsuo. Hwɛ owia da ano nwonwon sɛ ɛkyerɛ nsuo hia. Asintiɛ asase hia nsuo pii sen amane asase. Ntomato ne nnuan hia nsuo pii sen bankye.

instruction_en: What is the best method of irrigation for a small farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Nsuo to anammɔn bɛn na ɛyɛ ɔkorɔ wɔ afuo ketewa mu?
response_en: For small farms, drip irrigation is the most water-efficient method. Sprinkler irrigation is good for larger areas. Watering cans or buckets work for very small plots.
response_tw: Afuo ketewa ho, nsuo tete-tete kyɛ yɛ anammɔn a ɛfa nsuo nkyɛn pa ara. Sprinkler irrigation yɛ ɔkorɔ wɔ beae kɛse. Nsuo kyɛ anaa bucket yɛ adwuma wɔ afuo ketewa ketewa.

instruction_en: Can I use borehole water for irrigation?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Metumi de borehole nsuo to nnuaba so?
response_en: Yes, borehole water is generally good for irrigation. However some borehole water can be salty or have high mineral content which can damage crops over time. If possible, test the water at an agricultural lab.
response_tw: Aane, borehole nsuo yɛ ɔkorɔ wɔ nsuo to so. Nanso borehole nsuo bi tumi yɛ ɛwa anaa minerals wɔ mu dodo a ɛtumi sɛe nnuaba bere akyi. Sɛ wonya tumi a, hwɛ nsuo no wɔ agricultural lab.

instruction_en: How do I know when my maize is ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim sɛ m'aburow awie ase na ɛyɛ a megye no?
response_en: Maize is ready when the husks turn brown and dry, the silks are dark brown/black, and the kernels are hard. This is usually 90-120 days after planting depending on the variety.
response_tw: Aburow awie ase bere a ntwaho no dan ɛda na ayera, nhwiren no adan ɛda kɔkɔɔ/tuntum, na aba no ayɛ den. Eyi taa ba nnafoa 90-120 a wode aba to mu no akyi wɔ aba no ahodoɔ so.

instruction_en: What is the best time of day to harvest vegetables?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn wɔ da mu na ɛyɛ ɔkorɔ sɛ wogye nnuan?
response_en: Harvest vegetables in the early morning when temperatures are cool. This helps produce stay fresh longer. Avoid harvesting in midday heat. For leafy vegetables, harvest with dew still present for crispness.
response_tw: Gye nnuan owia da ano bere a owia hye no ayɛ tirim. Eyi ma nnuan no tena pɛ sen ara. Bɔ hwɛ wɔ awia mu ahuhuro mu gye nnuan. Nhaban nnuan ho, gye won a nsuyiri da won so ara da no ho nkae.
prompt: Question: What is the best time of day to harvest vegetables?
completion: Harvest vegetables in the early morning when temperatures are cool. This helps produce stay fresh longer. Avoid harvesting in midday heat. For leafy vegetables, harvest with dew still present for crispness.
prompt_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn wɔ da mu na ɛyɛ ɔkorɔ sɛ wogye nnuan?
completion_tw: Gye nnuan owia da ano bere a owia hye no ayɛ tirim. Eyi ma nnuan no tena pɛ sen ara. Bɔ hwɛ wɔ awia mu ahuhuro mu gye nnuan. Nhaban nnuan ho, gye won a nsuyiri da won so ara da no ho nkae.

instruction_en: When should I harvest tomatoes?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn na megye m'ntomato?
response_en: Harvest tomatoes when they are fully colored but still firm. For selling to distant markets, harvest at the breaker stage when they begin to change color so they ripen during transport without getting damaged.
response_tw: Gye ntomato bere a wɔanya wɔn rere na wɔda den ara. Sɛ wohwehwɛ tɔn wɔ akura a ɛwɔ akyiri a, gye wɔn bere a wɔredan wɔn rere na wɔrebɛware wɔ kwan so a wɔde kɔ efisɛ wontumi ntwam.

instruction_en: How do I harvest cassava properly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na megye m'bankye yie?
response_en: Cassava is usually harvested 9-18 months after planting. Cut the stem at 30-40 cm height then loosen the soil around the plant with a hoe and pull up the roots. Handle roots gently to avoid bruising.
response_tw: Bankye taa gye wɔ abosome 9-18 akyi a wode to mu. Twa tibo no wɔ sɛntimita 30-40 so, afei de akɔde fa asase a ɛhyia nnua no ho na twe ntini no sor. De ntini no pɛ efisɛ sɛ ehow a ɛma ɛsɛe ntɛm.

instruction_en: How do I harvest groundnuts without losing too many pods?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na megye nkatie a aba bi nko?
response_en: Harvest groundnuts when leaves start yellowing. Loosen the soil with a hoe first then pull the whole plant upward. Shake off excess soil and thresh on a clean tarpaulin. Dry pods in the sun for 3-5 days before storage.
response_tw: Gye nkatie bere a nhaban no redan sika. Fa akɔde fa asase no ho ansa, afei twe nnua no nyinaa sor. Woso asase a aka no afi na bu wɔ tarpaulin horo so. Yaw aba no wɔ owia mu nnafoa 3-5 ansa na waguina.

instruction_en: How do I harvest palm fruits properly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na megye abe aba yie?
response_en: Palm fruits are ready when they turn reddish-orange and some fruits start falling naturally from the bunch. Use a long-handled chisel or harvesting sickle to cut the bunch from the tree. Collect fallen fruits from the ground too. Process within 24-48 hours of harvest to avoid free fatty acid buildup which reduces oil quality.
response_tw: Abe aba wie ase bere a wɔdan kɔkɔɔ-ɛda na aba bi firi bɔ afi ne ho pɛ. Fa long-handled chisel anaa harvesting sickle twa bɔ no fi dua no ho. Gye aba a atu gu asase so nso. Yɛ adwuma wɔ dafe 24-48 akyi a wogye sɛ free fatty acid nbi dodo a ɛsua ngo ho pa.

instruction_en: When is mango ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere bɛn na mangoro awie ase sɛ megye?
response_en: Mangoes are ready to harvest when: the fruit changes color from green to yellow, orange, or red depending on variety; the fruit flesh near the seed turns yellow; the fruit gives slightly when gently pressed; and the fruit smells sweet near the stem. For commercial harvest, pick slightly before full ripeness so they ripen during transport.
response_tw: Mangoro awie ase bere a: aba no resen rere fi akyen kɔ hoho, ɛda, anaa kɔkɔɔ wɔ aba no ahodoɔ so; aba no nnompe a ɛbɛn aba no dan hoho; aba no tumi pii kakra bere a wode fa so pɛ; na aba no nhwiren foforo pue fi tibo ho. Tɔn ho, gye ansa na ɛawi pɛpɛɛpɛ na ɛware wɔ kwan so.

instruction_en: How do I harvest pepper without damaging the plant?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na megye mako a ɛnsɛe nnua no?
response_en: Harvest pepper using scissors or a sharp knife to cut the fruit with a small piece of stem — never pull the pepper off by hand as it damages the plant. Harvest when fruits are fully developed and have changed to their final color. Regular harvesting every 3-5 days encourages the plant to produce more fruits throughout the season.
response_tw: Gye mako fa scissors anaa sekan tew twa aba no a tibo kakra ka ho — mfa wo nsa twe mako afi nnua no ho da efisɛ ɛsɛe nnua no. Gye bere a aba no awi yie na wɔadan wɔn rere. Gye pɛ mmere 3-5 biara ma nnua no sɛ ɛkɔ ma aba dodo wɔ bere mu nyinaa.

instruction_en: How do I harvest and process shea nuts?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na megye ne yɛ adwuma wɔ nkuto aba?
response_en: Collect fallen shea fruits from the ground during the harvest season (May to August). Remove the sweet pulp by soaking and washing. Dry the nuts for 1-2 weeks in the sun. Crack the dried shells to get the kernels. Dry kernels further before storing or processing into shea butter. Never dry shea nuts on bare soil or over fire as this reduces butter quality.
response_tw: Gye nkuto aba a atu gu asase so wɔ gye bere mu (Ɔbɛnem kwan so Ɔsannaa). Yi asew a ɛda mu wɔ da wɔ nsuo mu na hohoro. Yaw aba no owia mu nnawotwe 1-2. Bubu nkuku a ayaw no sɛ wunya aba no mu ade. Yaw aba mu ade no bio ansa na waguina anaa yɛ nkuto ngo. Mfa nkuto aba yaw wɔ asase firi so anaa ogya so da efisɛ ɛsua ngo ho pa.

instruction_en: How do I know when my pineapple is ready to harvest?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim sɛ m'aborɔfo awie ase sɛ megye?
response_en: A pineapple is ready when: the skin changes from green to yellow starting at the base, the fruit gives a dull hollow sound when tapped, the fruit smells sweet and fragrant, the leaves at the top pull out easily, and the fruit is firm but gives slightly when pressed. Pineapples do not ripen further after harvest, so timing is critical.
response_tw: Aborɔfo awie ase bere a: foforo no resen fi akyen kɔ hoho fi ase, aba no su sɛ kɔkɔ bere a wode bɔ so, aba no nhwiren foforo pue na ɛyɛ dɛ, nhaban a ɛwɔ so no twe ba yie, na aba no yɛ den nanso tumi pii kakra. Aborɔfo nware bio akyi a wogye, enti bere yɛ titiriw pa ara.

instruction_en: How do I store maize to prevent aflatoxin?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meguina m'aburow na aflatoxin dze nka ho?
response_en: Dry maize properly to below 13% moisture before storage. Store in clean, dry, well-ventilated structures. Use hermetic bags to prevent insect infestation. Avoid mixing good grain with damaged grain.
response_tw: Yaw aburow yie na ɛsua 13% nsuku ansa na waguina. Guina wɔ fie a ɛhoro, yera, na ɛwɔ mframa pa. Fa hermetic bags sɛ adwummakers nni mu. Bɔ hwɛ ka aburo pa ne aburo a ɛasɛe no bom.

instruction_en: How long can I store cassava after harvesting?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Bere ahe na metumi guina bankye a megye no akyi?
response_en: Fresh cassava deteriorates within 24-72 hours after harvest. For longer storage, process into dried chips, gari, or flour. Waxing the roots can extend fresh storage to about a week. Keep in cool, shaded areas.
response_tw: Bankye foforo sɛe wɔ dafe 24-72 akyi a wogye no. Sɛ wohwehwɛ guina bere tenten a, yɛ no bankye chips a ayaw, gari, anaa mfurow. Waxing bankye no de tumi ma no tena pɛ bere koro nnawotwe mu. Wura no wɔ bae a ɛhuru na owia ntra mu.

instruction_en: How do I store yam properly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meguina m'bayerɛ yie?
response_en: Store yams in a cool, dark, well-ventilated area. Traditional yam barns work well. Avoid contact with soil and keep yams off the ground. Inspect regularly and remove any rotting tubers. Yams can last 4-6 months.
response_tw: Guina bayerɛ wɔ bae a ɛhuru, ɛsum na mframa pue mu yie. Amammerɛ bayerɛ guina sukuu yɛ adwuma pa. Bɔ hwɛ asase nka wɔn na ma wɔn na wɔntena asase so. Hwɛ wɔn so pɛ na yi ɔbayerɛ a ɛresɛe. Bayerɛ tumi tena yie abosome 4-6.
prompt: Question: How do I store yam properly?

instruction_en: How do I protect stored grain from rats and mice?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mekora m'aduan a maguina fi ɔkra ne okusie ho?
response_en: Keep the storage area clean and free of food scraps. Seal all holes and cracks in walls. Use snap traps or approved rodenticides. Store bags on raised platforms away from walls. Cats are also effective.
response_tw: Kora guina fie no horo na aduan asennua ntena mu. Popa tumtum ne ɛwɔwɔ nyinaa wɔ bɔ mu. Fa snap traps anaa dawuro a wɔagyei de kum ɔkra. Guina bags wɔ platform a ɛda sor tete akyi. Agyinamoa nso yɛ adwuma pa.
prompt: Question: How do I protect stored grain from rats and mice?
completion: Keep the storage area clean and free of food scraps. Seal all holes and cracks in walls. Use snap traps or approved rodenticides. Store bags on raised platforms away from walls. Cats are also effective.

instruction_en: How do I store tomatoes without refrigeration?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meguina m'ntomato a menhyɛ wɔn fridge mu?
response_en: Store tomatoes at room temperature away from direct sunlight. Do not stack heavily. Keep them stem-side down. Tomatoes last 1-2 weeks this way. For longer preservation, dry, can, or make tomato paste. Never refrigerate unripe tomatoes.
response_tw: Guina ntomato wɔ fie mu osu a ɛyɛ pɛ sɛ owia ntra wɔn so tɔtɔ. Mfa wɔn si so dodo. De wɔn si wɔn tibo fam. Ntomato tumi tena nnawotwe 1-2 eyi ho. Sɛ wohwehwɛ tena bere tenten a, yaw, kanta, anaa yɛ ntomato paste.
prompt: Question: How do I store tomatoes without refrigeration?
completion: Store tomatoes at room temperature away from direct sunlight. Do not stack heavily. Keep them stem-side down. Tomatoes last 1-2 weeks this way. For longer preservation, dry, can, or make tomato paste. Never refrigerate unripe tomatoes.

instruction_en: How do I make a simple low-cost hermetic storage?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meyɛ hermetic guina a ɛboɔ yɛ ketewa?
response_en: You can make low-cost hermetic storage using: triple-layer plastic bags where you place grain in an inner plastic bag, seal it, place in a second bag, seal it, then place in a third outer bag and seal. Or use metal drums with tight-fitting lids sealed with tape. Store off the ground on pallets. This method is very effective for 6-12 months of storage.
response_tw: Wobetumi yɛ hermetic guina a ɛboɔ yɛ ketewa wɔ: plastik bags mmiɛnsa a wode aduan si plastik bag mu, po so, de si bag abien mu, po so, afei de si bag mmiɛnsa mu na po so. Anoa fa metal drums a wɔn soro yɛ den a wɔde tape po so. Guina wɔ pallet so afi asase so. Anammɔn no yɛ adwuma yie pa ara wɔ guina abosome 6-12 mu.

instruction_en: How do I store dried fish or meat on the farm without a fridge?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meguina nsuomnam anaa nam a ayaw wɔ afuo so a menwɔ fridge?
response_en: Dry fish or meat thoroughly until it is very hard and dry. Wrap tightly in cloth or paper, then in a plastic bag. Store in a cool, dry, ventilated area away from moisture and insects. You can smoke the meat or fish to extend shelf life. Inspect weekly and re-dry immediately if any moisture is detected. Properly dried fish or meat can last 2-3 months.
response_tw: Yaw nsuomnam anaa nam no yie kosi sɛ ɛyɛ den na yera. De ntama anaa krataa sɛɛ mu yie, na afei de plastik bag sɛɛ. Guina wɔ bae a ɛhuru, yera, na mframa pue mu fi nsuo ne adwummakers ho. Wobetumi bu nam anaa nsuomnam no ewi sɛ ɛma no tena yie. Hwɛ so nnawotwe biara na yaw ntɛm sɛ nsuo wɔ mu. Nam anaa nsuomnam a ayaw yie tena abosome 2-3.

Nam anaa nsuomnam a ayaw yie tena abosome 2-3.

instruction_en: How do I prevent mold from growing on stored produce?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mema fungus amma ɛnda sor wɔ m'aduan a maguina?
response_en: Mold grows when moisture is present. Prevent it by: drying produce thoroughly before storage, storing in well-ventilated areas, using absorbent packaging like cloth or paper not plastic, checking stored goods regularly, removing any moldy produce immediately to prevent spread, keeping storage areas clean, and never mixing new produce with old.
response_tw: Fungus da sor bere a nsuo wɔ hɔ. Kora ase wɔ: yaw aduan no yie ansa na waguina, guina wɔ beae a mframa pue mu yie, fa packaging a ɛsuo nsuo te sɛ ntama anaa krataa na mfa plastik, hwɛ aduan a waguina pɛ, yi aduan a fungus ato ho ntɛm sɛ anntete, kora guina beae no horo, na mmfa aduan foforo ka aduan kedee bom da.
prompt: Question: How do I prevent mold from growing on stored produce?
completion: Mold grows when moisture is present. Prevent it by: drying produce thoroughly before storage, storing in well-ventilated areas, using absorbent packaging like cloth or paper not plastic, checking stored goods regularly, removing any moldy produce immediately to prevent spread, keeping storage areas clean, and never mixing new produce with old.
prompt_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mema fungus amma ɛnda sor wɔ m'aduan a maguina?
completion_tw: Fungus da sor bere a nsuo wɔ hɔ. Kora ase wɔ: yaw aduan no yie ansa na waguina, guina wɔ beae a mframa pue mu yie, fa packaging a ɛsuo nsuo te sɛ ntama anaa krataa na mfa plastik, hwɛ aduan a waguina pɛ, yi aduan a fungus ato ho ntɛm sɛ anntete, kora guina beae no horo, na mmfa aduan foforo ka aduan kedee bom da.

instruction_en: What is cold storage and can small farmers access it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na ntwene-nkuma guina yɛ na okuafo ketewa tumi nya ase anaa?
response_en: Cold storage is refrigerated storage that keeps produce at low temperatures to extend shelf life. Small farmers can access it through: farmer cooperatives that own shared cold storage facilities, government-run cold storage centers, private cold storage services that charge per crate or kilogram, mobile cold storage trucks, and evaporative coolers which are low-cost non-electrical cooling structures.
response_tw: Ntwene-nkuma guina yɛ aduan guina a wɔde ntwene nkuma so ma aduan tena yie bere tenten. Okuafo ketewa tumi nya ase wɔ: okuafo cooperative a wɔwɔ ntwene-nkuma guina bom, ɔman ntwene-nkuma guina centres, onipa hɔ ntwene-nkuma guina nnwuma a wɔsua crate anaa kilogram biara, mobile ntwene-nkuma guina trucks, ne evaporative coolers a ɛboɔ yɛ ketewa a wɔhyɛ electricity mu.

instruction_en: How do I store seeds safely for the next planting season?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na meguina m'aba yie ma bere a ɛto so?
response_en: Dry seeds thoroughly to less than 10% moisture. Sort and remove damaged, diseased, or small seeds. Place in airtight containers — glass jars, tins, or sealed plastic bags. Add a small packet of silica gel or a tablespoon of powdered milk wrapped in cloth to absorb moisture. Store in a cool, dark, dry place. Label with crop name, variety, and date. Test germination before planting season.
response_tw: Yaw aba no yie na ɛsua nsukuro 10%. Si na yi aba a asɛe, a yadeɛ aka, anaa a wɔkum. De aba to nkuku a apo so — glass jar, tin, anaa sealed plastik bag. De silica gel kotoku ketewa anaa powdered milk tablespoon baako a wɔde ntama sɛɛ no ka ho sɛ ɛsuo nsukuro. Guina wɔ bae a ɛhuru, ɛsum, na yera. Kyerɛw nnuaba din, aba din, ne da. Hwɛ aba pue ansa na bere aba.

instruction_en: Where can I sell my farm produce at a good price?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛhe na metumi de m'afuo nnuaba tɔn na nya boɔ pa?
response_en: Sell directly at local markets, to aggregators or food processing companies, or form a cooperative with other farmers. Register with government programs like PFJ which helps connect farmers to buyers.
response_tw: Tɔn tɔtɔ wɔ aguabasa a ɛbɛn wo, anaa wɔ aduan yɛ adeɛ kuo, anaa si cooperative ne okuafo foforɔ. Kyerɛw wo din wɔ ɔman adwuma te sɛ PFJ a ɛboa okuafo ayɔnkofa ne adeɛtɔfo.

instruction_en: How do I get a loan for farming?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menya ka sɛ meyɛ okuafo adwuma?
response_en: Access agricultural loans through GIRSAL, rural banks, microfinance institutions, and cooperatives. Have a farm business plan ready, register your farm officially, and consult your local MOFA office for guidance.
response_tw: Nya okuafo adwuma ka wɔ GIRSAL, ɔmansin ban, microfinance kuro, ne cooperatives mu. Wɔ wo afuo adwuma dwumadie plan, kyerɛw wo afuo din wɔ ɔman hɔ yie, na bisa wo MOFA ofisi wɔ beae a ɛbɛn wo.

instruction_en: How do I calculate my profit from farming?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na melem m'afuo ho mfaaso?
response_en: Profit equals Total Sales Revenue minus Total Costs. Track all expenses including seeds, fertilizer, labor, transport, and chemicals and all income from sales. Keep a simple notebook or use your phone to record daily expenses.
response_tw: Mfaaso yɛ sika nyinaa a wogye fi tɔn no minus sika nyinaa a wode. Kora sika nyinaa a wode ka ho te sɛ aba, ferefere, dwumadie, kwan so sika, dawuro, ne sika nyinaa a wogye fi tɔn. Kora krataa ketewa anaa fa wo fɔn de kora da biara ho sika.

instruction_en: Is it worth joining a farmer cooperative?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛsɛ sɛ meba okuafo kuo mu anaa?
response_en: Yes, cooperatives offer many benefits including group purchasing of inputs at lower prices, collective selling for better prices, access to credit, shared equipment, training, and stronger negotiating power with buyers and government.
response_tw: Aane, cooperative de mfaaso bebree brɛ: kuo to adenneɛ ketewa mu, kuo tɔn sɛ wanya boɔ pa, nya ka, fa egyapade bom, nyansapɔ, na kuo tumi yɛ kɛse sen wɔ adeɛtɔfo ne ɔman ho nsɛm mu.

instruction_en: How do I add value to my farm produce to earn more?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mede boɔ ka m'afuo nnuaba ho sɛ menya sika dodo?
response_en: Process raw produce into higher-value products: dry and package grains, make tomato paste, gari from cassava, or palm oil from palm fruits. Value addition reduces post-harvest losses and increases income.
response_tw: Yɛ aduan foforo no yɛ aduan a wɔtɔ boɔ kɛse: yaw na paka nnuaba a ayaw, yɛ ntomato paste, gari fi bankye, anaa ngo fi abe aba. Boɔ to so maa mpampa post-harvest mpoano ne ma sika dodo.

instruction_en: How do I transport my produce to market without spoilage?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mede m'nnuaba kɔ aguabasa a ɛnkɔ ase?
response_en: Use ventilated crates or baskets not plastic bags for vegetables and fruits. Harvest early morning and transport before midday heat. Avoid overloading to prevent bruising. Cover produce with wet sacks to keep cool. For grains use clean dry bags. Sell same day for perishables whenever possible.
response_tw: Fa afidie a mframa pue mu anaa akete na mfa plastik bags nsɛ nnuan ne aba. Gye owia da ano na de kɔ ansa na awia mu ahuhuro aba. Bɔ hwɛ de dodo hye mu sɛ ɛnhow. De ntama nte sɛɛ nnuaba so sɛ ɛkora wɔn ase huru. Nnuaba a ayaw ho, fa bags horo ne kraen.

instruction_en: What is a market information system and how does it help farmers?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na aguabasa nsɛm system yɛ na ɛboa okuafo sɛn?
response_en: A market information system provides farmers with current prices of crops at different markets so they can decide where and when to sell for the best price. In Ghana, services like Esoko, the Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX), and MOFA market information services provide this. Access via phone, radio, or internet. This prevents farmers from being cheated by middlemen who know prices but farmers don't.
response_tw: Aguabasa Nsɛm system de nnuaba boɔ a ɛwɔ aguabasa ahodoɔ so ma okuafo sɛ wɔtumi paw beae ne bere a wɔbɛtɔn sɛ wanya boɔ pa. Wɔ Ghana, nnwuma te sɛ Esoko, Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX), ne MOFA aguabasa nsɛm nnwuma de eyi brɛ. Nya ase wɔ fɔn, redio, anaa internet so. Eyi mma okuafo anko ase wɔ middlemen a wɔnim boɔ nanso okuafo nnim ntam.

instruction_en: What is crop insurance and can farmers in Ghana access it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na nnuaba ho nsirɛ yɛ na okuafo Ghana tumi nya ase anaa?
response_en: Crop insurance protects farmers against losses from drought, floods, pests, or other disasters by paying compensation when crops fail. In Ghana, the Ghana Agricultural Insurance Pool (GAIP) offers index-based weather insurance for farmers. Premiums are affordable and payouts are based on weather data not individual farm assessment. Contact MOFA or your cooperative to learn how to enroll.
response_tw: Nnuaba ho nsirɛ kora okuafo fi nnuaba ko a ɛba fi nsuo pa, nsuo tenten, adwummakers, anaa ɔkɔm foforɔ ho wɔ de sika brɛ wɔn bere a nnuaba nwu. Wɔ Ghana, Ghana Agricultural Insurance Pool (GAIP) de index-based weather insurance ma okuafo. Premium no boɔ yɛ ketewa na wɔde ɛwim nsɛm mmu ka ne afuo biara nhwɛ. Ka kyerɛ MOFA anaa wo cooperative sɛ wuhu ɛdeɛn na wosi wo din.

instruction_en: How do I price my farm produce fairly?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mesiesie m'afuo nnuaba boɔ yie?
response_en: To price fairly, calculate your total production cost per unit (cost of seeds, fertilizer, labor, transport, etc. divided by total harvest). Add a profit margin of at least 20-30%. Check current market prices for similar produce in your area. Your price should cover all costs plus give you profit. Never sell below your production cost even if market prices are low.
response_tw: Sɛ wohwehwɛ siesie boɔ yie a, lem wo adwuma ho sika nyinaa wɔ nnuaba biara so (aba, ferefere, dwumadie, kwan so sika, ne deɛ aka no, kyɛ nyinaa wɔ nnuaba a wogye no nyinaa so). Ka mfaaso a ɛsua 20-30% ho. Hwɛ boɔ a wɔde tɔn nnuaba te sɛ wo de wɔ wo beae. Wo boɔ sɛ akata sika nyinaa a wode na ma wo mfaaso. Mto da wɔ wo adwuma ho sika ase bere a aguabasa boɔ ayɛ sua.

instruction_en: What government support programs exist for farmers in Ghana?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɔman boa adwuma bɛn na ɛwɔ ho ma okuafo wɔ Ghana?
response_en: Key government programs for farmers in Ghana include: Planting for Food and Jobs (PFJ) which provides subsidized seeds and fertilizers; Rearing for Food and Jobs for livestock; Ghana Commercial Agriculture Project (GCAP) for commercial farmers; Youth in Agriculture Program; and the Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) loans. Contact your district MOFA office for current programs available in your area.
response_tw: Ɔman adwuma titiriw a ɛwɔ ho ma okuafo wɔ Ghana ne: Planting for Food and Jobs (PFJ) a ɛde aba ne ferefere a wɔatwa boɔ brɛ; Rearing for Food and Jobs ma mmoa; Ghana Commercial Agriculture Project (GCAP) ma tɔntɔn okuafo; Youth in Agriculture Program; ne Agricultural Development Bank (ADB) ka. Ka kyerɛ wo district MOFA ofisi ma adwuma a ɛwɔ hɔ wɔ wo beae saa ara.

Contact your district MOFA office for current programs available in your area.

instruction_en: What is the Ghana Commodity Exchange and how can I use it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na Ghana Commodity Exchange yɛ na ɛdeɛn na mefa di dwuma?
response_en: The Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX) is a formal marketplace where farmers can sell their produce at transparent, standardized prices. To use it: register as a member through an accredited warehouse operator, store your certified grain in a GCX-accredited warehouse, receive a warehouse receipt that you can sell at the exchange or use as collateral for a bank loan. It is especially good for maize, soya, and sorghum.
response_tw: Ghana Commodity Exchange (GCX) yɛ aguabasa a ɛwɔ ho yie a okuafo tumi tɔn wɔn nnuaba wɔ boɔ a wɔada adi ne a wɔasiesie. Sɛ wohwehwɛ fa di dwuma a, kyerɛw wo din wɔ accredited warehouse operator ho, guina wo nnuaba a wɔakyerɛw wɔ GCX-accredited warehouse mu, nya warehouse receipt a wobetumi tɔn wɔ exchange no mu anaa de kɔ ban ho sɛ collateral. Ɛyɛ adwuma yie pa ara ma aburow, soya, ne ɔtɛ.

instruction_en: How do I manage my farm during drought?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehwɛ m'afuo so bere a nsuo pa?
response_en: During drought, mulch heavily to conserve soil moisture, irrigate if possible, choose drought-tolerant crop varieties, and avoid adding nitrogen fertilizer which increases water demand. Harvest early-maturing varieties before drought peaks.
response_tw: Bere a nsuo pa no, de mulch to asase so yiye sɛ nsukuro wo mu, de nsuo to nnua so sɛ wonya tumi, paw nnuaba aba a ɛtumi nsuo pa so, na bɔ hwɛ de nitrogen ferefere to sɛ ɛma nnuaba hia nsuo dodo. Gye nnuaba a wɔware ntɛm ansa na nsuo pa yɛ dodo.
prompt: Question: How do I manage my farm during drought?
completion: During drought, mulch heavily to conserve soil moisture, irrigate if possible, choose drought-tolerant crop varieties, and avoid adding nitrogen fertilizer which increases water demand. Harvest early-maturing varieties before drought peaks.

instruction_en: Heavy rains flooded my farm. What should I do?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Osu tenten ma nsuo hyɛɛ m'afuo mu. Dɛn na meyɛ?
response_en: After flooding, make drainage channels to remove excess water quickly. Allow soil to drain before entering with heavy machinery. Check plants for root rot and remove severely damaged ones. Apply potassium fertilizer to help plants recover. Be alert for increased fungal diseases after flooding.
response_tw: Nsuo tu akyi, yɛ mmɔnkyemmonkyem sɛ nsuo no pue ntɛm. Ma asase no ntena ansa na wode egyapade kɛse ba mu. Hwɛ nnuaba ntini ho sɛ wɔasɛe na yi a wɔasɛe kɛse. De potassium ferefere to so sɛ ɛboa nnuaba no sane aba.

Check plants for root rot and remove severely damaged ones. Apply potassium fertilizer to help plants recover. Be alert for increased fungal diseases after flooding.
prompt_tw: Asemmisa: Osu tenten ma nsuo hyɛɛ m'afuo mu. Dɛn na meyɛ?
completion_tw: Nsuo tu akyi, yɛ mmɔnkyemmonkyem sɛ nsuo no pue ntɛm. Ma asase no ntena ansa na wode egyapade kɛse ba mu. Hwɛ nnuaba ntini ho sɛ wɔasɛe na yi a wɔasɛe kɛse. De potassium ferefere to so sɛ ɛboa nnuaba no sane aba.

instruction_en: Strong winds broke my maize plants. Can they recover?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Mframa tumi buu m'aburow nnua. Wɔtumi sane aba anaa?
response_en: Lodging from wind can be managed by carefully straightening and mounding soil around fallen plants within 24-48 hours. However if the stem is broken recovery is unlikely. To prevent lodging choose short-stalked varieties and avoid excessive nitrogen.
response_tw: Wobetumi hwɛ nnua a mframa ato wɔn ase no ase wɔ de asase de si wɔn ho pɛ wɔ dafe 24-48 mu. Nanso sɛ tibo no apae a, ɛnyɛ ɔkorɔ sɛ wɔsane ba. Sɛ wohwehwɛ kora ase a, paw aba a wɔn tiboo tia na kora nitrogen ferefere ase.

instruction_en: How do I know when the rains will start?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim bere a osu reba?
response_en: Watch for natural signs such as flowering of certain trees, behavior of birds, and increased humidity. Check Ghana Meteorological Agency forecasts on radio, TV, or their website. Local MOFA extension officers often share seasonal forecasts. Planting too early before rains establish risks seed loss.
response_tw: Hwɛ nhwiren foforo nsɛm te sɛ nnua bi abode, anomaa biribi, ne huhu a ɛresor. Hwɛ Ghana Meteorological Agency amanneɛ wɔ redio, TV, anaa wɔn website so. MOFA extension officers a ɛbɛn wo no taa kyɛ bere amanneɛ. To aba ntɛm dodo ansa na osu reba retwe aba no sɛe.

instruction_en: How do I manage weeds effectively without spending too much?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehwɛ nhaban a ɛntia asɛm yie a mennya sika koraa?
response_en: Control weeds by timely hand weeding in the first 3-4 weeks after planting, using mulch to suppress weeds, and applying pre-emergence herbicides at planting. The first weeding must be done within 2-3 weeks after crop emergence.
response_tw: Hwɛ nhaban foforo ase wɔ nnafoa 3-4 a aburow pue akyi no mu, fa mulch kora nhaban foforo ase, na de herbicide to ansa na nnuaba pue. Nhaban foforo tia a ɛdi ɛkan no yɛ wɔ nnafoa 2-3 mu a nnuaba pue akyi.

instruction_en: What is intercropping and is it beneficial?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na ɛyɛ intercropping na ɛboa?
response_en: Intercropping means growing two or more crops together on the same land. For example, maize with cowpea. Benefits include better land use, the legume adds nitrogen to the soil, reduced pest pressure, and income from two crops.
response_tw: Intercropping kyerɛ sɛ wokura nnuaba abien anaa dodo wɔ asase koro so. Nhwɛso so, aburow ne abɔdweɛ. Mfaaso ho ne: asase ho dwuma pa, abɔdweɛ de nitrogen ma asase, adwummakers hia sua, na nnuaba abien firi sika.

instruction_en: How many times should I weed my farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Mmmere ahe na msɛ meyɛ nhaban foforo tia wɔ m'afuo mu?
response_en: Weed at least twice per season — first weeding within 2-3 weeks of planting and second weeding 4-6 weeks after planting. After this, crop canopy usually shades out weeds. Maize is most sensitive to weeds in the first 30-40 days after planting.
response_tw: Tia nhaban foforo mmere abien pɛ wɔ bere biara — nhaban tia a ɛdi ɛkan wɔ nnawotwe 2-3 a wode aba to mu akyi na nhaban tia a ɛto so abien wɔ nnawotwe 4-6 akyi. Eyi akyi, nnuaba nhaban taa sɛɛ nhaban foforo ase.

instruction_en: How do I keep records on my farm?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mekora m'afuo nsɛm?
response_en: Keep a simple farm diary or notebook. Record planting dates, inputs purchased and costs, labor hired and paid, crop activities, harvest quantities, and sales. Simple mobile apps or voice memos on your phone can help record activities easily.
response_tw: Kora afuo krataa anaa nsɛmpoma ketewa. Kora aba to da, adenneɛ a wotɔ ne won boɔ, dwumadie a wohwɛ ne sika a wode twaa, afuo adwuma, nnuaba a wogye, ne tɔn nsɛm. Mobile apps ketewa anaa kasa nkrataa wɔ wo fɔn no tumi boa wo kora nsɛm yie.

instruction_en: How do I know the size of my farm in acres?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na menim m'afuo boɔ wɔ acres mu?
response_en: Measure your farm using a measuring tape, GPS app like Google Maps, or by counting paces where 100 paces by 100 paces is approximately 0.2 acres. Alternatively your local MOFA office can help measure your farm.
response_tw: Sus wo afuo wɔ susudua, GPS app te sɛ Google Maps anaa OsmAnd, anaa kan tɔntɔn a tɔntɔn 100 ne tɔntɔn 100 yɛ bɛn acres 0.2. Anoa, wo MOFA ofisi a ɛbɛn wo no tumi boa wo su wo afuo.

instruction_en: How do I manage farm labor efficiently?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na mehwɛ afuo dwumadie yie?
response_en: Manage farm labor by: planning activities in advance and knowing how many workers you need for each task, hiring skilled workers for technical tasks and using family labor for routine work, paying fair wages on time to retain good workers, organizing communal labor (nnoboa) with neighboring farmers to reduce costs, tracking time spent on each activity to identify inefficiencies, and using appropriate tools to reduce manual labor.
response_tw: Hwɛ afuo dwumadie wɔ: dwumadie siesie ansa na ɛba na nim obi dodo a wohia ma adwuma biara, fa obi a onim adwuma ma adwuma a ɛhia nyansam na fa abusua adwuma ma adwuma a wɔtaa yɛ, tua dwumadie sika a ɛfata bere a ɛsɛ sɛ wokora adwumfo pa, si nnoboa ne okuafo a wɔbɛn wo sɛ mpampa sika, kora bere a wɔde yɛ adwuma biara so sɛ wuhu beae a enni adwuma pa, na fa adeɛ a ɛfata di dwuma sɛ mpampa nsa adwuma.
prompt: Question: How do I manage farm labor efficiently?

instruction_en: What is a farm map and why should I make one?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na afuo map yɛ na ɛsɛ sɛ meyɛ baako adɛn?
response_en: A farm map is a simple drawing or diagram showing your farm layout including field boundaries, crops in each area, water sources, access roads, trees, and infrastructure. It helps you: plan crop rotations, track what was planted where, plan irrigation and drainage, apply for land certificates, and communicate with extension officers and banks. You can draw it by hand or use free GPS apps on your phone.
response_tw: Afuo map yɛ kyerɛwee anaa diagram ketewa a ɛkyerɛ wo afuo anammɔn a mu wɔ afuo nkyɛn, nnuaba a ɛwɔ beae biara mu, nsuo, kwan, nnua, ne adwumafie. Ɛboa wo sɛ: siesie nnuaba sesae, kora nsɛm a wode aba to mu wɔ beae bɛn, siesie nsuo to ne nsuo pue anammɔn, bisa asase krataa, na kasa ne extension officers ne ban. Wobetumi kyerɛw wo nsa so anaa fa GPS apps daa wɔ wo fɔn so.

instruction_en: How do I reduce post-harvest losses from poor transportation?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Ɛdeɛn na metumi sua mpoano a ɛba fi kwan so anammɔn bɔne ho?
response_en: Reduce transport losses by: harvesting at the right maturity, grading and sorting produce before transport, using appropriate packaging (ventilated crates for fresh produce, strong sacks for grains), loading carefully to prevent crushing, traveling during cool hours (early morning or night), using roads that avoid excessive vibration, and selling as close to the farm as possible to reduce transport distance.
response_tw: Sua kwan so mpoano wɔ: gye nnuaba bere a ɛsɛ, si ne yi nnuaba ansa na wede kɔ, fa packaging a ɛfata (afidie a mframa pue mu ma nnuaba foforo, kotoku den ma nnuaba a ayaw), de adwuma yie sɛ ɛnhow, kɔ bere a ɛhuru (owia da ano anaa anadwo), fa kwan a wonyi nnuaba no dodo, na tɔn a ɛbɛn afuo so ara sɛ sua kwan asukyerɛ.

instruction_en: What is precision farming and can smallholders practice it?
instruction_tw: Asemmisa: Dɛn na precision farming yɛ na okuafo ketewa tumi yɛ?
response_en: Precision farming means using data and technology to apply exactly the right amount of inputs at the right time and place. Smallholders can practice basic precision farming by: soil testing different parts of the field and applying fertilizer based on results, using mobile apps to track crop growth, monitoring pest and disease hotspots and targeting treatment, and keeping detailed farm records to identify what works best.
response_tw: Precision farming kyerɛ fa data ne technology di dwuma sɛ de adenneɛ a ɛfata to bere a ɛsɛ na beae a ɛsɛ. Okuafo ketewa tumi yɛ basic precision farming wɔ: hwɛ asase ahorow afuo mu na de ferefere to wɔ adwuma ho, fa mobile apps di dwuma sɛ kora nnuaba da sor, hwɛ adwumakers ne yadeɛ beae na hyɛ dawuro hɔ pɛ, na kora afuo nsɛm mu yiye sɛ wuhu dea yɛ adwuma pa.
